spec_match.rationale: Calls `Iterator.concat()` directly across the iterable types the TC39 proposal targets — arrays, Sets, Map.values(), strings, generators — and a side-by-side eager-spread comparison that surfaces the allocation difference the spec is designed to avoid.

interactive.rationale: Five preset buttons run real `Iterator.concat()` calls with output logging, plus a paired eager-vs-lazy split panel with separate run buttons — a thorough demonstrator.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises arrays, Sets, generators, and Map entries; the page delivers all four plus strings and an iterator-helpers chain.

edge_cases.rationale: Polyfill fallback for older browsers is implemented and the support banner flips accordingly. But no demo of: an exception thrown mid-sequence (does the next source still iterate?), an iterator that throws on its first call to next(), or the difference between `Symbol.iterator` and `Symbol.asyncIterator` (the spec is sync-only — worth saying explicitly).

design_system.rationale: Variables drive most styling, but inline `style="font-size:0.78rem;background:var(--bg-stone)..."` on the eager/lazy compare panes is bulky and would be cleaner as a class — minor consistency issue. Buttons follow the global rule.

openQuestions.title: Demo error propagation through the sequence
openQuestions.detail: If a generator throws on the third yield, what happens to the subsequent concatenated sources? The spec says iteration stops and propagates. Add a small example that triggers a mid-sequence throw and shows the resulting behaviour so authors can build robust pipelines.

openQuestions.title: Explicitly call out the sync-only contract
openQuestions.detail: `Iterator.concat()` works on sync iterables only — there is a separate `AsyncIterator.concat` proposal. The page should explicitly note this so authors don't mix `ReadableStream` or `for await` patterns and get confused.

openQuestions.title: Measure heap allocation difference
openQuestions.detail: The eager-vs-lazy comparison shows the conceptual difference but doesn't quantify it. Add `performance.memory.usedJSHeapSize` deltas (with a feature-detection caveat) to make the savings concrete on large-iterable runs.

summary: Comprehensive demonstrator covering every iterable type the spec targets, with a working polyfill fallback. Add error propagation, an explicit sync-only callout, and a heap-allocation deltabar to round it out.
